title: Kinematic timing transmission — phase and thermal study
summary: Investigate phase accuracy, backlash sensitivity, thermal growth, manufacturability.

included: thermal expansion effects on center distance

problem_statement.need: Quantify timing-drive phase error, backlash sensitivity, thermal growth, and manufacturability for the current architecture.
problem_statement.why: The subsystem must maintain phase accuracy across the operating envelope without creating an unmanufacturable tolerance stack.

scenarios.description: Steady-state hot operation at maximum ambient temperature.

success_criteria.statement: Maximum phase error stays within allowable budget across the operating scenario.
success_criteria.rationale: Phase error is the primary functional risk.

constraints.statement: Center distance adjustment must remain inside the OEM packaging window.

provenance.rationale: Packaging envelope is fixed by adjacent assemblies.

assumptions.statement: A lumped thermal model is acceptable for the first-pass study.
assumptions.validation_intent: Compare the first-pass prediction against a finer-grained model before release.
assumptions.impact_if_false: Thermal growth may be underpredicted and phase error budget could be invalid.

responses.description: Predicted phase error at the output shaft.

deliverables.description: Parameterized kinematic and thermal analysis plan with executable artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Analysis plan references the governing variables, constraints, and verification path.

deliverables.description: Verification report covering phase-error limits and blocked assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Verification outcome is explicit and references the generated analysis artifacts.

inputs.description: Layout PDF or drawing extract for the timing system.

code_guidance.summary: Implementation should stay inside the analysis tooling for the timing study.
implementation_hints: Prefer deterministic calculations over heuristic approximations.

forbidden_actions: No deletion of safety-critical tests without human approval.
